title: Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil

seo.title: How to Make Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil — Easy Italian Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil. This easy Italian recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

description: Imagine sinking your teeth into a slice of authentic Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil, a true taste of the Italian Riviera. This beloved flatbread boasts a delightfully crisp crust, a soft airy interior, and a fragrant aroma of fresh rosemary and high-quality olive oil. Perfect as an appetizer, side, or light meal, it brings the warmth of Genoa right to your table with every delicious bite.

instructions.title: Activate the Yeast
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the warm water, sugar, and active dry yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until the mixture becomes foamy, indicating the yeast is active.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: Add the flour and fine sea salt to the yeast mixture. Pour in 60ml of the extra virgin olive oil. Mix with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. Transfer the dough to a lightly floured surface.

instructions.title: Knead and First Rise
instructions.text: Knead the dough for about 10-15 minutes until it is smooth, elastic, and no longer sticky. Form it into a ball, place it in a large, well-oiled bowl, turning once to coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until doubled in size.

instructions.title: Shape the Focaccia
instructions.text: Gently punch down the risen dough and transfer it to a 9x13 inch baking sheet generously drizzled with olive oil. Using your fingertips, gently press and stretch the dough to fill the pan, creating characteristic dimples across the surface. Try not to deflate it too much.

instructions.title: Second Rise and Topping
instructions.text: Drizzle the remaining 20ml of olive oil over the dough, ensuring it pools slightly in the dimples. Scatter the fresh rosemary leaves and flaky sea salt evenly over the surface. Cover loosely and let it rise again for 30-45 minutes.

instructions.title: Bake to Golden Perfection
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 220C (425F). Bake the focaccia for 20-30 minutes, or until it is golden brown on top and cooked through. The crust should be crisp and the interior soft and airy.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, remove the focaccia from the oven and carefully transfer it to a wire rack to cool for at least 10-15 minutes before slicing. This allows the internal structure to set. Serve warm or at room temperature.

tips: For an extra crispy bottom, preheat your baking sheet in the oven before placing the dough on it. Be careful when handling.
Use a high-quality extra virgin olive oil. Its flavor is prominent in this recipe and greatly affects the final taste.
Focaccia is best enjoyed fresh. If you have leftovers, store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 days. Reheat briefly in the oven for best results.
article:
Dive into the heart of Italian baking, and you'll find a masterpiece of simplicity and flavor: Focaccia Genovese. More than just a flatbread, it's a culinary hug, a testament to the power of quality ingredients and time-honored techniques. Imagine a golden-brown crust, crisp yet yielding, giving way to an interior so soft, airy, and riddled with delightful pockets, all infused with the aromatic essence of rosemary and the rich fruitiness of premium olive oil. This is the magic of Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil, a staple that transcends mere food to become an experience.

Originating from Genoa, the capital of Liguria, this focaccia is distinct from its cousins across Italy. It’s not just about the dimples; it’s about the perfect balance of texture and taste, a savory canvas that celebrates the bounty of the Mediterranean. Whether enjoyed as a breakfast treat with coffee, a midday snack, or alongside a glass of wine, this focaccia is a versatile star that promises to transport your taste buds straight to the sun-drenched Ligurian coast.

## What is Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil?

At its core, Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is a yeast-leavened flatbread characterized by its unique texture and flavor profile. Unlike many focaccias that might be heavily topped, the Genovese version is celebrated for its elegant simplicity. It features a thin, exceptionally crisp bottom crust, a wonderfully soft and airy crumb, and a surface punctuated by distinctive dimples that hold a savory emulsion of olive oil, water, and sea salt. This emulsion is crucial, creating tiny wells of flavor and contributing to the focaccia's characteristic salty crispness.

The "Rosemary Olive Oil" in its name isn't just a descriptor; it's a declaration of its essential components. Fresh rosemary, finely chopped, is generously scattered over the dough before baking, lending its piney, aromatic notes. Extra virgin olive oil, especially a good quality Ligurian olive oil, is not merely an ingredient but a foundational element, used both in the dough and as a generous coating. It contributes to the focaccia's golden hue, its moist crumb, and its irresistible aroma. The traditional Genovese style is deliberately sparse with toppings, allowing the inherent flavors of the dough, oil, rosemary, and salt to shine through unimpeded, creating a truly authentic and unforgettable Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting the perfect Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil requires just a handful of simple ingredients, but their quality is paramount.

*   **Strong Bread Flour (approx. 500g):** High-protein bread flour is recommended for its ability to develop gluten, leading to that characteristic chewy yet airy texture. All-purpose flour can work in a pinch, but the results may be slightly less robust.
*   **Warm Water (approx. 400-450ml):** The exact amount can vary slightly depending on your flour and environment. Aim for around 100-105°F (38-40°C) to activate the yeast effectively.
*   **Active Dry Yeast (7g or 2 ¼ teaspoons):** Essential for leavening. Instant yeast can also be used, often without proofing.
*   **Fine Sea Salt (10g or 1 ½ teaspoons for the dough, plus more for the brine and topping):** Salt is crucial for flavor and for controlling yeast activity.
*   **Extra Virgin Olive Oil (at least 80-100ml, plus more for the pan and topping):** This is non-negotiable. Invest in a high-quality, flavorful extra virgin olive oil. It’s the soul of the focaccia.
*   **Fresh Rosemary (2-3 sprigs):** Chopped finely, its fragrant needles are sprinkled over the top. Dried rosemary can be substituted, but fresh is vastly superior.
*   **Flaky Sea Salt (for topping):** A sprinkle of flaky sea salt on top provides a delightful textural crunch and bursts of salinity.

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps to bring your Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil to life:

1.  **Activate the Yeast:** In a large bowl, combine the warm water with a pinch of sugar (optional, helps activate yeast) and the active dry yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy, indicating the yeast is active.
2.  **Mix Dry Ingredients:** In a separate bowl, whisk together the bread flour and fine sea salt.
3.  **Combine Wet and Dry:** Pour the activated yeast mixture into the flour mixture. Add 2 tablespoons of extra virgin olive oil to the bowl. Mix with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy, sticky dough forms. Do not overmix; just ensure all flour is incorporated.
4.  **Knead (or Stretch and Fold):** This dough is very wet. You can knead it on a lightly oiled surface for 5-7 minutes until it becomes somewhat smoother and more elastic, though it will remain sticky. Alternatively, for a no-knead approach, cover the bowl and let it rest for 30 minutes, then perform a series of "stretch and folds" every 30 minutes for 2 hours (total 4-5 folds). To do this, lightly oil your hands, grab a portion of the dough from the edge, stretch it upwards, and fold it over to the center. Rotate the bowl and repeat until you've gone around the entire dough.
5.  **First Rise (Bulk Fermentation):** Lightly oil the bowl, place the dough back in, turn it once to coat, and cover with plastic wrap or a damp towel. Let it rise in a warm place for 1.5 to 2 hours, or until it has doubled in size.
6.  **Prepare the Pan:** Generously coat a 9x13 inch (or similar size) baking pan with 3-4 tablespoons of extra virgin olive oil.
7.  **Transfer and Dimple:** Gently transfer the risen dough into the prepared pan. Using your fingertips, gently stretch the dough to fill the pan, being careful not to deflate it too much. Once stretched, press your fingers firmly into the dough, creating deep dimples that go almost to the bottom of the pan.
8.  **Second Rise:** Cover the pan loosely with plastic wrap or a damp towel and let the dough rise again for 30-45 minutes, or until noticeably puffy.
9.  **Prepare the Brine/Emulsion:** In a small bowl, whisk together 2 tablespoons of extra virgin olive oil, 2 tablespoons of water, and ½ teaspoon of fine sea salt until emulsified.
10. **Final Topping:** Pour the olive oil-water emulsion evenly over the dimpled dough. Sprinkle generously with finely chopped fresh rosemary and flaky sea salt.
11. **Bake:** Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C). Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the focaccia is golden brown on top and beautifully crisp on the bottom.
12. **Cool:** Once baked, immediately transfer the Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil from the pan to a wire rack to cool slightly. This prevents the bottom from becoming soggy.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that authentic, irresistible Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil involves a few key details:

*   **Quality Olive Oil is King:** This cannot be stressed enough. A good quality extra virgin olive oil not only imparts flavor but also contributes to the focaccia's texture and golden crust. Don't skimp here.
*   **High Hydration Dough:** Focaccia dough is meant to be wet and sticky. Resist the urge to add more flour than specified, as this will result in a dense, dry focaccia. Embrace the stickiness; it's what creates those beautiful air pockets.
*   **Gentle Handling:** Once the dough has risen, handle it as gently as possible. You want to preserve all those precious air bubbles that contribute to its light, airy crumb.
*   **Deep Dimples:** Don't be shy with your dimpling! Press your fingertips firmly into the dough, almost to the bottom of the pan. These dimples are essential for holding the olive oil brine and creating a wonderfully uneven, textured surface.
*   **The Brine is the Secret:** The olive oil and water emulsion, poured over the dough just before baking, is a hallmark of Genovese focaccia. It's responsible for the incredibly crispy, salty crust and a moist interior.
*   **Don't Overbake:** Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil should be golden brown, not dark brown. Overbaking will dry it out and make it tough.
*   **Cool on a Rack:** Always transfer the focaccia to a wire rack immediately after baking. This allows air to circulate around the bottom, preventing condensation and ensuring the crust remains crisp.

## Serving Suggestions

Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is incredibly versatile and delicious in many ways:

*   **Plain and Warm:** The purest way to enjoy it, fresh from the oven, perhaps with just a drizzle of extra olive oil for dipping.
*   **With Coffee:** In Genoa, it's a traditional breakfast item, often dipped into cappuccino.
*   **Appetizer or Snack:** Serve alongside a platter of cured meats (prosciutto, salami), cheeses (pecorino, fresh mozzarella), and marinated olives.
*   **Soup and Salad Companion:** Its rustic charm makes it a perfect accompaniment to hearty soups or fresh, vibrant salads.
*   **Sandwich Bread:** Slice it horizontally and fill it with your favorite sandwich fillings – think roasted vegetables, grilled chicken, or classic Italian deli meats.
*   **Bruschetta Base:** Cut into smaller pieces, lightly toast, and top with fresh tomatoes, basil, and garlic for a delightful bruschetta.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is a masterpiece in itself, you can explore variations:

*   **Different Herbs:** While rosemary is traditional for Genovese, you could experiment with fresh thyme, oregano, or a mix of Italian herbs for a different flavor profile.
*   **Added Toppings:** For a less traditional but equally delicious focaccia, consider adding other toppings before baking. Thinly sliced onions, cherry tomatoes, olives (kalamata or green), or minced garlic can be pressed into the dimples.
*   **Cheese:** A sprinkle of grated Parmesan or Pecorino Romano can be added with the rosemary for a cheesy twist.
*   **Gluten-Free:** Use a high-quality gluten-free bread flour blend (often containing xanthan gum) as a substitute for wheat flour. Be aware that the texture may differ slightly.
*   **Whole Wheat:** Replace a portion (up to 50%) of the bread flour with whole wheat flour for a nuttier flavor and denser crumb, though this deviates from the classic Genovese texture.
*   **Less Oil:** While not recommended for authentic Focaccia Genovese, you can reduce the amount of olive oil used in the pan or topping if you prefer a lighter result, but you will sacrifice some flavor and crispness.

Embrace the joy of baking this simple yet profound Italian bread. With its golden crust, airy crumb, and aromatic embrace of rosemary and olive oil, Focaccia Genovese Rosemary Olive Oil is more than just food – it’s a taste of Italy, a slice of sunshine, and a true delight for the senses. Buon appetito!
